title: Brighter Super joins sector’s strong year for investment returns
full_text:
Brighter Super has reported positive returns across its diversified investment options for the 12 months to 30 June 2026, with its Growth option returning 10.18 per cent for accumulation members and 11.25 per cent for pension members.
Brighter Super’s MySuper option returned 9.56 per cent for accumulation members, placing it alongside several large industry funds that generated returns around the 9 per cent mark over the financial year.
The fund’s Balanced option returned 8.60 per cent for accumulation members and 9.41 per cent for pension members, while Conservative Balanced returned 7.31 per cent and 8.10 per cent respectively.
Indexed Balanced delivered 8.16 per cent and 8.86 per cent, Stable returned 5.97 per cent and 6.69 per cent, and Secure produced returns of 3.34 per cent and 4.05 per cent.
The results add to a strong year for Australia’s superannuation sector, with many funds posting high single-digit or double-digit returns despite market volatility fuelled by changing economic conditions, shifting interest rate expectations and geopolitical uncertainty.
While returns generally moderated from the exceptional gains recorded in FY25, diversified portfolios continued to benefit from resilient global equity markets.
Chief executive Kate Farrar said the fund had continued to deliver for members amid an uncertain backdrop.
“Despite a year marked by market volatility, Brighter Super’s investment options continued to deliver resilient outcomes for members.”
Across the sector, funds have pointed to diversified portfolios and resilient sharemarkets as key drivers of returns over the past financial year, even as investors navigated fluctuating interest rate expectations and global events.
Farrar said investment performance was only one part of building retirement savings, with fees also playing a significant role in long-term outcomes.
“While investment performance plays an important role in growing retirement savings, fees matter too. By combining competitive long-term returns with low fees, we’re helping members keep more of their money invested for the future.”
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation and Investments
page_title: Brighter Super joins sector's strong year for investment returns - Super Review
